title: Executive Calendar Conflict Resolution
instruction: Three VPs — Marcus Chen, Avery Garcia, and Elena Garcia — have each sent a meeting request email to the CEO. Find all three meeting request emails (search for "Meeting Request" in the subject). Also find the email from Elena Garcia with subject containing "CEO Schedule — Confirmed" which lists the CEO's existing commitments. One of the three meeting requests conflicts with an existing commitment (same day, overlapping time). Reply to that VP declining the meeting (reply to the sender only — do not Reply All, because the thread CC's board members). Reply to the other two VPs accepting their meetings. Forward all three meeting request emails to elena.garcia@company.com. Star the conflicting VP's email for follow-up.
eval_check_descriptions: Decline reply sent to conflicting VP only
Accept replies sent to both non-conflicting VPs
All three meeting requests forwarded to EA

emails.subject: Re: Meeting Request — Reschedule
emails.body:
Please reschedule the board meeting to next week instead.

Something came up and I need to move all my meetings.

Thanks,
Avery

emails.subject: CEO Schedule — Confirmed for March 24-28
emails.body:
Hi Avery,

Here is the CEO's confirmed schedule for the week:

- Monday: 9:00 AM – 10:30 AM — Leadership Team Standup
- Tuesday: 2:00 PM – 3:30 PM — Board Prep Review
- Wednesday: 10:00 AM – 11:00 AM — Investor Call
- Thursday: 1:00 PM – 2:00 PM — All-Hands Rehearsal
- Friday: 3:00 PM – 4:30 PM — Week Retrospective

Please flag any conflicts.

Thanks,
Elena

emails.subject: Notes from the partner sync
emails.body:
Passing along the current draft so you have it in one place.

Nothing looks blocked right now, but we still need one decision on timing. Let me know if you want the spreadsheet version as well.

Thanks,
Elena

emails.subject: Meeting Request — Budget Discussion
emails.body:
Hi Avery,

I'd like to schedule a meeting with the CEO to discuss Budget Discussion.

Proposed time: Thursday, 3:00 PM – 4:00 PM.

Please confirm or suggest an alternative.

Thanks,
Elena

emails.subject: Meeting Request — Strategy Review
emails.body:
Hi Avery,

I'd like to schedule a meeting with the CEO to discuss Strategy Review.

Proposed time: Tuesday, 2:30 PM – 3:30 PM.

Please confirm or suggest an alternative.

Thanks,
Avery

emails.subject: Meeting Request — Product Deep Dive
emails.body:
Hi Avery,

I'd like to schedule a meeting with the CEO to discuss Product Deep Dive.

Proposed time: Wednesday, 1:00 PM – 2:00 PM.

Please confirm or suggest an alternative.

Thanks,
Marcus

emails.subject: Reminder on document review timing
emails.body:
Passing along the current draft so you have it in one place.

The team is aligned on the main path and only needs confirmation on the follow-up items. Let me know if you want the spreadsheet version as well.

Thanks,
Jordan

emails.subject: Revised budget worksheet
emails.body:
I pulled the loose ends into one note for easier review.

The team is aligned on the main path and only needs confirmation on the follow-up items. No response needed unless you want anything reordered.

Thanks,
Marcus

The team is aligned on the main path and only needs confirmation on the follow-up items. If you want, I can turn this into a cleaner summary before the meeting.

Thanks,
Marcus

We covered the Q3 metrics review and talent pipeline update.

Thanks,
Nina
